message:
beads: sync issues.jsonl after flywheel_connectors-lopoh.1 close

Beads tracking file drift from multi-agent session work — records
the closed state of the Ed25519 connector-signing bead
(flywheel_connectors-lopoh.1) with its final notes referencing the
fwc supply-chain signing path (manifest signature + attestation
emission, registry verification, remote cargo check -p fwc pass).

Committing so other agents' br/bd sync stays consistent.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1626,7 +1626,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.1","title":"[Registry] Sign connector binaries with Ed25519 and generate supply chain attestations","description":"Implement the connector signing workflow: 1) Build a connector in release mode, 2) Compute BLAKE3 hash of the binary, 3) Sign manifest + binary hash with publisher Ed25519 key, 4) Generate in-toto attestation linking source commit to binary hash, 5) Package as: binary + manifest.toml + signature + attestation. Verify the signing with fcp-registry's existing verification code. Output: a signed connector package suitable for registry upload. This should be scriptable as a cargo xtask or a standalone tool in crates/fcp-conformance/.","notes":"Implemented fwc supply-chain signing path with manifest signature + attestation emission and registry verification. Remote rch cargo check -p fwc passed earlier in session; targeted sign_bundle test passed remotely. Workspace cargo fmt --check is currently noisy on unrelated files outside this bead.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:50.276480Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T05:27:19.462356Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T05:27:19.462181Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crypto","registry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:50.276480Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.2","title":"[Registry] Create minimal local registry server for connector install/update flow","description":"Build a minimal HTTP registry server that serves signed connector packages. Functionality: 1) Serve connector manifest by ID, 2) Serve connector binary by ID + platform/arch, 3) Return checksums and signatures for verification, 4) Support version listing and latest-version resolution. This does NOT need to be production-grade - it's the minimum viable registry to prove the install/update flow works. Can be implemented as a simple axum server or even a static file server with a known directory structure. Location: crates/fcp-registry/src/bin/ or a new crate.","notes":"Completed: local registry server implemented; remote rch proofs green for fcp-registry local_registry_ tests and fwc sign_bundle_emits_registry_verified_package.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleMoose","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:50.990700Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T07:34:56.237549Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T07:34:56.237380Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["registry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:50.990700Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:51.704147Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.3","title":"[Registry] Prove fwc install and fwc update against the local registry with full verification","description":"With the local registry running and signed packages available: 1) Run 'fwc install <connector>' and verify it downloads, verifies signature, checks checksum, and installs the binary, 2) Run 'fwc update <connector>' and verify it detects a newer version and upgrades, 3) Attempt install with a tampered binary - MUST fail signature verification, 4) Attempt install with wrong platform/arch - MUST fail platform check, 5) Verify supply chain attestation is checked if policy requires it. This proves the README's supply chain verification pipeline works end-to-end.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleMoose","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:52.225551Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T09:19:34.695872Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T09:19:34.695697Z","close_reason":"Completed: registry install/update proof added; targeted rch check/test passed; broad rch clippy blocked by repeated worker sync timeouts to vmi1149989","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","registry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:52.225551Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:52.960837Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","title":"[Registry] Verify GitHub Actions release workflow produces cross-platform binaries","description":"The .github/workflows/release.yml (11KB) exists but has never been demonstrated to produce artifacts. Verify: 1) Trigger the release workflow (manually or via tag), 2) Verify it builds for Linux x86_64 + aarch64, macOS x86_64 + aarch64, 3) Verify binaries are signed, 4) Verify checksums are generated, 5) Verify a GitHub Release is created with all artifacts attached. If the workflow fails, fix it. This is the distribution pipeline that makes FCP installable by users outside this repository.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:53.483847Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T16:08:53.925690Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","registry"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:53:53.483847Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2747,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose verification of .github/workflows/release.yml (2026-04-18):

WORKFLOW STRUCTURE — CORRECT:
1. Triggers: push tags v*, workflow_dispatch with version input ✓
2. Concurrency: group=release, cancel-in-progress=false ✓
3. Build matrix: 5 platforms (Linux x64+ARM64, macOS x64+ARM64, Windows x64) ✓
4. Packages: tar.xz (Unix), zip (Windows) ✓
5. Checksums: SHA256 per-artifact + combined SHA256SUMS.txt ✓
6. Signing: Sigstore cosign keyless (OIDC) ✓
7. Release: softprops/action-gh-release@v2 with download table ✓
8. Publish: dry-run only (actual crates.io publishes commented out) ✓

FINDINGS:
- Workflow has NEVER been triggered (gh run list --workflow=release.yml returns empty)
- No tags or releases exist in the repo
- Only 4 connectors built (anthropic, discord, openai, telegram) — ~146 others omitted
- fcp-host and fcp-cron binaries not included in release (only fwc CLI)
- Publish step is dry-run only — crates.io tokens not configured
- ubuntu-24.04-arm runner may not be available in all GitHub tiers

CANNOT VERIFY WITHOUT TRIGGER:
- Whether the workflow actually succeeds on GitHub Actions (nightly toolchain + workspace deps)
- Whether cosign signing works with OIDC in this repo's configuration
- Whether the 45-minute timeout is sufficient for cross-compilation

RECOMMENDATION: Cannot close this bead without actually triggering the workflow. This requires creating a tag or using workflow_dispatch, which is a user action (pushing to the remote). The workflow definition appears structurally correct but is untested.","created_at":"2026-04-19T02:47:21Z"},{"id":2749,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose deep verification of release.yml (2026-04-19):

CRITICAL BUG FOUND AND FIXED (7f24fd41):
All 5 release targets (fwc, fcp-anthropic, fcp-discord, fcp-openai, fcp-telegram) depend on two external repos via local path:
- asupersync at ../../../asupersync (7 crates: fcp-async-core, fcp-host, fcp-telemetry, fcp-tailscale, fcp-graphql, discord, slack)
- toon_rust at ../../../toon_rust (fwc only, via 'tru' package)
These paths would not resolve on GitHub Actions CI. Fixed by adding shallow git clones in all 3 build jobs.

LOCAL VERIFICATION (macOS ARM64):
1. Release build fwc: OK (7.3 MB, 2m32s)
2. Release build fcp-anthropic: OK (4.0 MB)
3. Release build fcp-discord: OK (5.4 MB)
4. Release build fcp-openai: OK (4.1 MB)
5. Release build fcp-telegram: OK (4.9 MB)
6. tar.xz packaging: OK (5 archives, 1.5-3.1 MB each)
7. SHA256 checksums: OK (verified round-trip)
8. Combined SHA256SUMS.txt: OK

REMAINING ITEMS (cannot verify without triggering workflow):
- Cross-platform builds (Linux x64/ARM64, macOS x64, Windows x64)
- Cosign OIDC keyless signing (requires GitHub Actions OIDC provider)
- ubuntu-24.04-arm runner availability (GA since Jan 2026)
- 45-minute timeout sufficiency for cold-cache builds
- softprops/action-gh-release@v2 release creation

RECOMMENDATION: Workflow is now structurally ready for a test trigger via workflow_dispatch. The user should run: gh workflow run release.yml -f version=0.0.1-test","created_at":"2026-04-19T03:18:03Z"},{"id":2759,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"Handing back on 2026-04-19: latest GitHub Actions release run is still the stale queued run 24631366023 on commit 0daecf680f70ce95ea86f89ccf4bb162773108e3 (queued		Release	Release	main	workflow_dispatch	24631366023	46m45s	2026-04-19T14:27:42Z). It is not green, so the bead cannot be closed. Local fixes now exist for both blockers discovered in that run: Linux dbus build deps in .github/workflows/release.yml (commit 79a58bda) and Windows Rust 2024 HANDLE/unsafe extern fixes in crates/fcp-sandbox/src/windows.rs (commit 2d2fd519). The current blocker is that GitHub has not executed a new post-fix release run yet; rerun release.yml after those commits are on the remote branch, then verify all platform legs, signing, checksums, and release asset publication before reclosing lopoh.4.","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:14:27Z"},{"id":2760,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"Investigating the still-stale release dispatch. The only live run remains 24631366023 on 0daecf6; Linux and Windows failures in that run are the pre-fix failures, and both x86_64-apple-darwin jobs are still queued more than an hour later on macos-13. I am patching .github/workflows/release.yml to route the x86_64-apple-darwin target through macos-14 instead so the release proof no longer depends on a separate Intel macOS runner becoming available.","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:29:39Z"},{"id":2763,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-04-19 follow-up: latest `gh run list --workflow=release --limit 3` still shows only stale queued run 24631366023, so lopoh.4 is not closable yet. The requested fallback beads are unavailable in the current graph: d5yeb is already closed and z1nkz.4 does not exist. Pulling the current ready set now for the next actionable task while release proof remains blocked on a fresh post-fix dispatch.","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:52:06Z"},{"id":2764,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"Current state after recheck: gh run list --workflow=release --limit 3 still shows only queued run 24631366023 on head 0daecf680f70ce95ea86f89ccf4bb162773108e3. None of the known release-fix commits are on any remote branch yet: 79a58bda (Linux dbus deps), 2d2fd519 (Windows sandbox compile), 7eeff127 (route x86_64-apple-darwin target through macos-14). lopoh.4 therefore remains blocked on the absence of any fresh post-fix release dispatch, not on a newly observed runtime failure.","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:52:33Z"},{"id":2766,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"Additional CI blocker fixed on 2026-04-19: .github/workflows/release.yml previously used top-level concurrency  with . Because the only existing dispatch 24631366023 is stuck queued on old head 0daecf6, that policy can pin the entire release lane and prevent a fresh post-fix retry on main from replacing it. I changed the workflow to  and  so a new manual dispatch on main cancels the stale queued run instead of waiting behind it forever.","created_at":"2026-04-19T15:58:20Z"},{"id":2768,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-04-19 hand-back: gh run list --workflow=release --limit 3 still shows only stale queued run 24631366023 on old head 0daecf680f70ce95ea86f89ccf4bb162773108e3. That run still reflects the pre-fix Linux dbus, Windows sandbox, and Intel-mac queue failures; no fresh post-fix release dispatch exists yet to verify the committed workflow fixes. Requested alternates are not actionable in the current graph: flywheel_connectors-d5yeb is already closed and flywheel_connectors-z1nkz.4 does not exist.","created_at":"2026-04-19T16:03:07Z"},{"id":2769,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-04-19 hand-back: gh run list --workflow=release --limit 3 still shows only stale queued run 24631366023 on old head 0daecf680f70ce95ea86f89ccf4bb162773108e3. That run still reflects the pre-fix Linux dbus, Windows sandbox, and Intel-mac queue failures; no fresh post-fix release dispatch exists yet to verify the committed workflow fixes. Requested alternates are not actionable in the current graph: flywheel_connectors-d5yeb is already closed and flywheel_connectors-z1nkz.4 does not exist.","created_at":"2026-04-19T16:03:38Z"},{"id":2772,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-04-19 blocker hand-back: reclaimed the bead and rechecked GitHub Actions. `gh run list --workflow=release --limit 3` still shows only queued run 24631366023 from 2026-04-19T14:27:42Z on head 0daecf680f70ce95ea86f89ccf4bb162773108e3. There is no fresh post-fix release run to inspect, so there is no new failure beyond the stale pre-fix Linux dbus / Windows sandbox / Intel-mac queue issues already addressed locally. Specific blocker: none of the CI-fix commits have been exercised on remote because no new release dispatch has started on a branch containing 79a58bda, 2d2fd519, 7eeff127, and 7d2fbe7d. This bead should be retried only after a fresh `release.yml` run is launched from a remote ref containing those commits.","created_at":"2026-04-19T16:08:23Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lopoh.5","title":"[Registry] Test supply chain rejection: tampered binary, expired signature, wrong platform all caught","description":"Negative tests for supply chain verification: 1) Flip a byte in signed binary -> install MUST fail with signature verification error, 2) Sign with expired timestamp -> install MUST fail with expired attestation error, 3) Present wrong platform binary (linux on macOS) -> install MUST fail with platform mismatch, 4) Present binary with capabilities not in zone ceiling -> install MUST fail with capability subset violation, 5) All failures should produce structured errors (not panics or generic messages). These prove the README's supply chain verification pipeline actually CATCHES attacks, not just that it verifies valid packages.","notes":"Progress 2026-04-07: strengthened negative coverage to assert structured errors for tampered binary, wrong platform, capability ceiling violation, SLSA insufficiency, missing attestation evidence, and untrusted builder; added explicit expired-attestation rejection path in fcp-registry via AttestationEvidence.expires_at + RegistryError::AttestationExpired, plus integration and unit tests for expiry; MockTufVerifier now also rejects expired TUF metadata.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lszk","title":"[FCP2] Connector Library: V2-Compliant Connectors","description":"# [FCP2] Connector Library: V2-Compliant Connectors

## Goal
Build a library of production connectors that **fully comply** with the FCP V2 platform:
- single-zone binding
- default deny via capabilities
- revocation freshness enforcement
- correct receipts and audit events
- sandbox + network constraints
- no secrets on disk; no secret logging

This epic intentionally assumes the **core platform** exists first.

## Entry criteria (what must exist before connectors are real)
Connectors should not begin until these are available:
- `[FCP2] Connector SDK (Rust)`
- `[FCP2] Connector Manifest (TOML)`
- `[FCP2] Policy Engine` (DecisionReceipts + reason codes)
- `[FCP2] Sandbox + Egress Proxy`
- `[FCP2] MeshNode` (enforcement boundary)
- `[FCP2] Registry + Supply Chain` (install/verify)
- `[FCP2] Conformance` (golden vectors + fuzz + compliance runner)

## Definition of Done
- Each connector has:
  - a manifest with explicit capabilities + sandbox profile
  - a conformance test suite (unit + integration with mocks)
  - E2E compliance run output
  - performance checks where applicable
  - no real external API calls in unit tests

## Notes
- Backwards compatibility is not a goal. Implement the V2 model directly.
- Mechanical compliance checking is part of the conformance suite.

## Success Criteria
- Every connector epic follows `flywheel_connectors-lszk.5` (base beads `.1`..`.4`, state model, NetworkConstraints, etc.).
- Every connector has mock-only unit/integration coverage per `flywheel_connectors-h32`.
- Tier-1 connectors (Telegram, Discord, OpenAI, Anthropic) each have:
  - a complete manifest/capability map
  - provisioning automation (where applicable)
  - passing E2E compliance runs (`flywheel_connectors-e3i9` + `flywheel_connectors-1n78.21.5`).
- No connector requires hard-coded pricing tables; usage metrics are emitted and policy can compute costs externally.
